channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-352] obdecho don't work with verify mode
item.description:
obdecho doesn't work on verify mode.

[root@rh5-64 utils]# [root@rh5-64 obdfilter-survey]# ./obdfilter-survey
Mon May 23 07:38:32 EEST 2011 Obdfilter-survey for case=disk from rh5-64.shadowland
ost 2 sz 33554432K rsz 1024K obj 2 thr 2 write Terminated
[root@rh5-64 obdfilter-survey]# verify=1 ./obdfilter-survey
Mon May 23 07:39:01 EEST 2011 Obdfilter-survey for case=disk from rh5-64.shadowland
ost 2 sz 33554432K rsz 1024K obj 2 thr 2 write 1336928.79 ERROR rewrite 1075489.49 ERROR read

LustreError: 19113:0:(debug.c:119:block_debug_check()) test_brw: id 0x2 offset 1040384 end id: 0xdeadbeef00c0ffee != 0x2
LustreError: 19113:0:(debug.c:102:block_debug_check()) test_brw: id 0x2 offset 1044480 off: 0xdeadbeef00c0ffee != 0xff000
LustreError: 19113:0:(debug.c:107:block_debug_check()) test_brw: id 0x2 offset 1044480 id: 0xdeadbeef00c0ffee != 0x2
LustreError: 19113:0:(debug.c:114:block_debug_check()) test_brw: id 0x2 offset 1044480 end off: 0xdeadbeef00c0ffee != 0xff000
item.environment: RHEL6, or RHEL5 with any lustre 2.x
item.summary: obdecho don't work with verify mode

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: Xyratex MRP-131
LU-1400 has now landed so I think that this ticket can be resolved again
obdfilter-survey doesn't work well with patch landed

Landing this patch breaks obdfilter-survey, interesting that while patch testing the obfilter-survey wasn't ran even. Check Maloo results before landing and after. Test 1c exit with error, test 2a stop working as well:
https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sets/7a782004-81ed-11e1-aa68-525400d2bfa6

I've created LU-1400 ticket for that already and today find out that this started when current patch was landed.

Integrated in lustre-dev » x86_64,client,el6,inkernel #340
LU-352 obdfilter: fix obdfilter-survey to work on verify mode. (Revision c13874ddf2b5a27bcac79b1db10cf426f487613c)

Result = SUCCESS
Oleg Drokin : c13874ddf2b5a27bcac79b1db10cf426f487613c
Files :

  • lustre/obdfilter/filter_internal.h
  • lustre-iokit/obdfilter-survey/obdfilter-survey

  • lustre/obdfilter/filter.c
  • lustre/obdecho/echo_client.c
  • lustre/tests/obdfilter-survey.sh

http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,598
just killl that method and obd_brw will return ENOSUPP.
In that case, let's return an error code in filter_brw() so that we won't be confused by it again.

if you will be fix filter_preprw to use a pages from obdecho you will stop to use a zero-copy feature in mdraid because it is need to have mapping with special flag.

so - i think better to force obdecho to use existent code (obd_preprw + obd_commitrw) for obdfilter, it 3 lines fix in obdecho.

/** obdfilter don't supported obd_brw now, simulate via prep_commit */
test_mode = (long)data->ioc_pbuf1;
if (ed->ed_next == NULL)

{ test_mode = 3; data->ioc_plen1 = data->ioc_count; }

switch(test_mode) {

or you need to force obdfilter-survey to use a 'p' flag in case 'disk'.

+# obdfilter need to use prep+commit mode
+# osc need to use kbrw mode
+testcase2mode()

{ + case $case in + disk) echo "p$1";; + *) echo "g";; + esac +}
  • test_brw $count `testname2type $test` q $pages ${thr_per_obj}t${first_obj} &"
    + test_brw $count `testname2type $test` q $pages ${thr_per_obj}t${first_obj} `testcase2mode $pages` &"

so you will have same behavior without touch any lines in code.

Indeed.

It seems filter_brw() won't work at all, because the page pointer will be cleared by filter_preprw anyway. It seems more reasonable to me to fix this problem by revising filter_preprw(). What do you think?

i think on both.

without extra changes in obdfilter-survey,
obdecho uses a obd_brw() call to read or to write a data to the obdfilter device.
but after landing R/O cache obd_preprw don't want to have allocated pages from TLS, and allocated pages himself. So pointers to pages submitted by obdecho is replaced by allocated in obd_preprw and none data copped to obdecho module.
in that case obdecho see a poisoned buffer
LustreError: 19113:0:(debug.c:119:block_debug_check()) test_brw: id 0x2 offset 1040384 end id: 0xdeadbeef00c0ffee != 0x2
instead of data from write phase.

that can be fixed by two ways.
one way is adding a special parameter in lctl test_brw command to tell obdecho uses obd_preprw + obd_commitrw instead of obd_brw().
second way to add a extra check in obdecho module "is lower device is obdfilter" and force to use a obd_preprw + obd_commitrw in that case.

My submitted fix implement a both of them.

Jay it's enough ?

Hi Shadow,

Can you please tell me a bit more about where is the problem? Is the problem happening at read or write phase?

Thanks Shadow - I saw. However, even if we have a contributed fix, someone still needs to be responsible for checking it gets tested, inspected and landed. I just noticed that HongChao is already working on this area of code for another ticket, so reassigning to him.

Peter, i have submitted fix to Gerit.
http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,598

Niu

Could you please look at this one when you are able to

Thanks

Peter

In fact fix is easy
lctl / obdecho has a two cases to work with lower fs. one is prep + commit, second is brw.

BRW methods is broked for obdecho and for 'disk' case we need to choose a prep + commit method.

I spent some time to make investigation.
That badnes a result of r/o cache landing, so now obd_preprw allocate a new pages for read from mapping and that is overwrite a page pointers submited from obdecho.
Also it's broke a 0-copy testing.

Hi Jay

Thanks for review a obdecho code. I will look to obdecho sources and try to fix it, because it's blocking bug for us.

Hi Shadow - long time no see.
After checking the code, I saw the verification only happens in read. However, I notice that there seemed erroneous on write phase from your log, let's make sure the write works firstly.
